data.narrative:
Ramon.Space is a Series B-stage space & aerospace company that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies in the same sector and stage, including Aalyria, Hubble Network, and Sift.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $50M total raised as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $95M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables (Aalyria at $1.3B, Hubble Network at $400M, and Sift at $274M) yields a median of $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples is not applicable absent disclosed revenue figures.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods produces a low estimate of $81M, a median of $248M, and a high of $460M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around which peer set most closely reflects Ramon.Space's stage and market position. Stage-progression suggests a more conservative valuation anchored to historical capital deployment; peer-median captures current market pricing for comparable late-stage space ventures but depends heavily on the relevance of the three selected comps. Key sensitivities include revenue trajectory visibility, competitive positioning within commercial space infrastructure, and near-term funding environment conditions for the sector.
